name: Kimi K2.5
archDisplay: 1T-parameter MoE, 32B active/token, 384 experts (8 routed + 1 shared), 61 layers (1 dense), MLA attention, 160K vocab, SwiGLU; native multimodal via 400M-param MoonViT vision encoder
note: Predecessor to Kimi K2.6 (whose own docs explicitly call it 'Successor to K2.5'); Moonshot's first native-multimodal flagship, built via continual pretraining atop Kimi-K2-Base on ~15T mixed vision+text tokens. Introduced native image/video understanding and the 'Agent Swarm' research-preview multi-agent orchestration (self-directed, up to 100 sub-agents, ~1,500 coordinated tool-call steps), trained with Parallel-Agent Reinforcement Learning (PARL). Vendor claims near-frontier coding/reasoning/vision performance versus GPT-5.2 and Claude Opus 4.5-class closed models; independent Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index score was 38/100 (v4.1 methodology, ranked #14/95 open+closed models at time of measurement), corroborating strong but not universally frontier-topping performance. As of July 2026 it has been superseded as Moonshot's flagship by K2.6 but remains live and served via Moonshot's own API (platform.moonshot.ai) and third-party hosts (OpenRouter, Fireworks, etc.).

specSources.priceInput/priceOutput: https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/kimi-k2-5 (cross-checked against Moonshot AI's own provider row on https://openrouter.ai/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5)

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified, Kimi K2.5 (Thinking); independently corroborated by aireleasetracker.com's aggregator, which lists the same 76.8%
SWE-bench Pro, harder/agentic variant distinct from base swe-bench
GPQA-Diamond, avg@8, 96k-token completion budget; Artificial Analysis's independent re-run scored 87.9, closely matching
MMMU-Pro, official protocol (input order preserved, images prepended)

Humanity's Last Exam, full set (text+image), with tool use enabled (search/code-interpreter/web-browsing), used here for comparability with the sibling K2.6 entry's tool-enabled HLE figure; vendor also reports 30.1 without tools. Artificial Analysis's independent non-tool run scored 29.4, close to the vendor's own non-tool figure
Terminal-Bench 2.0, default Terminus-2 agent framework, evaluated in non-thinking mode due to context-management incompatibility with Terminus-2

BrowseComp with context management; vendor also reports 60.6 single-agent (no ctx mgmt) and 78.4 in the 'Agent Swarm' multi-agent research-preview mode. 74.9 used here for rough comparability with the sibling K2.6 entry's single-agent-style figure
CharXiv (RQ), Kimi K2.5 Thinking, avg@3, max-tokens=64k, from the official Kimi K2.5 tech blog benchmark appendix table.
MathVision, Kimi K2.5 Thinking, avg@3, no python tool use.
CyberGym, Kimi K2.5 Thinking, averaged over 5 runs using Moonshot's in-house coding-eval framework.

Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard v3, Kimi K2.5 (both 'Thinking' and base rows show identical 64.5). Data attributed on-page to Artificial Analysis ('Benchmarks from Artificial Analysis'), independently measured rather than vendor self-reported. Not previously tracked for this model.

Arena.ai (formerly LMArena) Text Arena Overall Elo for 'kimi-k2.5-thinking' -- rank 51/374, 59,902 votes, as of the Jul 13, 2026 leaderboard snapshot (confirmed via the page's own embedded leaderboard data, not just a text summary). Corpus entry's note prose already cites an approximate 'Elo ~1522' figure for the sibling K2.6 model but neither K2.5 nor K2.6 had a formal arena result row; this adds K2.5's.
SuperCLUE 中文大模型基准测评 2025 年年度报告 (2025 annual report, published 2026-02-05): best domestic open-source model, rank 4 globally.
Independently corroborated by Vals.ai's own re-run (~84.3% / 84.335% raw) for 'Kimi K2.5 Thinking' on its MMMU benchmark page.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed as a real, released, and still-served model (not a rumor or vendor teaser). Architecture (1T total/32B active, 384 experts [8 routed + 1 shared], 61 layers [1 dense], MLA attention, 160K vocab, SwiGLU, 400M-param MoonViT vision encoder) and license (Modified MIT) verified directly on the official Hugging Face model card (huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5) and cross-checked against the official Kimi tech blog (kimi.com/blog/kimi-k2-5), which present identical spec tables. Release date January 27, 2026 independently corroborated by TechCrunch's same-day coverage, OpenRouter's model listing ('Released Jan 27, 2026'), Artificial Analysis's model page (independently states 'released on January 27, 2026'), and multiple third-party trackers (aireleasetracker.com, pricepertoken.com, benchlm.ai) that all agree on the date. Pricing ($0.60/M input cache-miss, $3.00/M output, ~$0.10/M cache-hit) confirmed on Artificial Analysis's independently measured provider data and matches the 'Moonshot AI' direct-provider row shown on OpenRouter exactly. Benchmark scores are taken from the official Kimi K2.5 blog/HF results table (Kimi K2.5 'Thinking' configuration, the config the vendor itself reports as the flagship numbers): SWE-Bench Verified (76.8) is independently corroborated by a third-party tracker (aireleasetracker.com) reporting the identical 76.8%; GPQA-Diamond (87.6) is closely corroborated by Artificial Analysis's own independent re-run (87.9); HLE is reported here using the tool-augmented full-set score (50.2) for comparability with the sibling K2.6 entry's HLE figure, and Artificial Analysis's independent non-tool HLE run (29.4) is in the same range as the vendor's own non-tool figure (30.1), i.e. no red flags of inflated self-reporting. Overall Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index = 38/100 (v4.1 composite of GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA-Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR), independently measured and broadly consistent with (though less flattering than) Moonshot's own selective benchmark table — mirroring the same self-reported/independent gap pattern documented on the sibling K2.6 corpus entry. As of July 2026 the model has been superseded as Moonshot's flagship by Kimi K2.6 (whose own note/docs explicitly call it 'Successor to K2.5') but remains live and actively served today via Moonshot's own API (platform.moonshot.ai) and third-party hosts (OpenRouter, Fireworks, ModelRun, Chutes, etc.), so it is catalogued as status=released rather than deprecated.

2026-07-16: Resolved the four core-benchmark gaps flagged for this entry (mmlu, math, humaneval, mmmu). Checked (a) official launch materials -- the kimi.com/blog/kimi-k2-5 tech blog, the moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5 Hugging Face model card, and the full official technical report (Kimi Team, "Kimi K2.5: Visual Agentic Intelligence", arXiv:2602.02276v1, Table 4, fetched directly and grepped for benchmark names) -- and (b) independent leaderboards, specifically Vals.ai (whose model page for "Kimi K2.5 Thinking" was pulled via server-rendered HTML to read the underlying bar-chart data attributes directly) and Artificial Analysis's Kimi K2.5 model page. (1) MMLU: no base-MMLU score for K2.5 in any of the above; only MMLU-Pro (already tracked at 87.1) is reported. Predecessor Kimi K2 (0711) did report MMLU=89.5, so Moonshot evidently dropped the legacy MMLU eval starting with K2.5/K2.6/K2-Thinking in favor of MMLU-Pro -- consistent with this corpus's own benchmarks.json description of MMLU as "largely saturated at the frontier." Verdict: omitted_no_evidence. (2) MATH: no MATH/MATH-500 score for K2.5 anywhere checked (no MATH row in arXiv Table 4); Vals.ai's own MATH-500 benchmark page is explicitly banner-marked "ARCHIVED BENCHMARK -- Since performance on this benchmark has saturated, we no longer run this benchmark on new model releases." Predecessor K2 reported MATH=97.4, reinforcing that later Kimi releases deliberately dropped it post-saturation rather than this being a sourcing gap. Verdict: omitted_no_evidence. (3) HumanEval: no HumanEval/HumanEval+ score for K2.5 in any official material; Vals.ai's own LiveCodeBench page states outright that "benchmarks like HumanEval have become saturated" and HumanEval is not part of its current tracked suite -- Moonshot's own coding evals for K2.5 are LiveCodeBench and SWE-Bench instead. Verdict: omitted_no_evidence. (4) MMMU: found and added. The official arXiv technical report's Table 4 reports "MMMU (val)" = 84.3 for Kimi K2.5, a genuinely distinct benchmark from the already-tracked MMMU-Pro (78.5) and omitted from the shorter blog post. Independently corroborated by Vals.ai's own re-run of "Kimi K2.5 Thinking," whose page's embedded bar-chart data shows an MMMU accuracy of 84.335% (~84.3%), closely matching the vendor figure -- added as self-reported with the independent cross-check noted.

2026-07-17: Second-pass deep research targeting the three still-missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval) using source types NOT covered by the prior pass (which had exhausted official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, and llm-stats.com). Checked all 6 of the newly-suggested source categories:

(1) Stanford HELM -- fetched HELM Lite, HELM Classic, HELM Capabilities, and HELM Instruct leaderboards directly via a live browser session (their tables are client-rendered so a plain HTML fetch returns nothing; had to actually load and read the rendered DOM). HELM Lite and HELM Classic (which do run genuine 'MMLU' and 'MATH'/'HumanEval' scenarios) list only models through ~DeepSeek v3/Claude 3.5/GPT-4o vintage -- neither leaderboard has been refreshed to include any Kimi model at all, let alone K2.5. HELM Capabilities (MMLU-Pro/GPQA/IFEval/WildBench/Omni-MATH) does include the sibling 'Kimi K2 Instruct' (MMLU-Pro COT 0.819, Omni-MATH 0.654) but never K2.5, and doesn't run base MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval for any model. Verdict: HELM has no K2.5 data of any kind, ruling out this source cleanly.

(2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar baseline citations -- re-examined the official Kimi K2.5 technical report (arXiv:2602.02276) and cross-checked its full benchmark-category list via an independent full-text Chinese summary (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/2011049995713926336, a technical-report digest citing the same arXiv ID), which enumerates every eval category the paper actually reports: reasoning/general (HLE, AIME 2025, HMMT 2025, IMO-AnswerBench, GPQA-Diamond, MMLU-Pro, SimpleQA Verified, AdvancedIF, LongBench v2), code (SWE-Bench family, Terminal-Bench 2.0, PaperBench, CyberGym, SciCode, OJBench, LiveCodeBench), agentic, image, video, and computer-use categories -- none of which include base MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval. Searched arXiv/web for other 2026 model papers (GLM-5, DeepSeek V4, Qwen3.5/3.6, MiniMax) using Kimi K2.5 as a baseline comparison point; found only secondary aggregator/comparison sites (e.g. morphllm.com's 'GLM-5 vs Kimi K2.5' page, surfaced via search summary as claiming a base 'MMLU 92% vs 85%' gap) with no traceable primary source -- direct fetch of that page returned HTTP 429 twice and could not be verified, and the number directly contradicts the official paper's own reported eval list (which omits base MMLU entirely), so it was rejected as unverifiable/likely fabricated rather than added. No genuine arXiv baseline table with real MMLU/MATH/HumanEval numbers for Kimi K2.5 was found.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs -- found and inspected github.com/yongzx/kimi-k2.5-safety-evaluation (the repo backing arXiv:2604.03121, 'An Independent Safety Evaluation of Kimi K2.5'): contains only safety-domain harnesses (Petri, self-replication, evaluation-awareness, AgentHarm, PsychosisBench), no capability benchmarks. Also found a public lm-evaluation-harness gist, but it targets 'Kimi-K2-Thinking' (a different sibling model, not K2.5) and only runs hellaswag/gsm8k_cot, not MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. No committed results.json for K2.5 on any of these three benchmarks was found on GitHub.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators -- OpenRouter's Kimi K2.5 benchmarks page surfaces only MMLU-Pro among knowledge evals (no base MMLU). Sigmabench (OpenCode agent leaderboard) lists K2.5 with only its own custom Sigmascore/Accuracy/Consistency/Speed metrics. Scale AI's Seal-0 leaderboard ranks Kimi K2.5 #1 (0.574) but Seal-0 is an agentic-search benchmark, unrelated to MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. LiveBench.ai's current (2026-06-25) release tracks Kimi K3, Kimi K2.6 Thinking, and Kimi K2.7 Code under Moonshot AI but not K2.5 itself, and its 7 categories (Reasoning/Coding/Agentic Coding/Mathematics/Data Analysis/Language/Instruction Following) are LiveBench's own custom-contamination-free tasks, not literal MMLU/MATH/HumanEval even when a model is present. PapersWithCode has no dedicated Kimi K2.5 page/leaderboard entries.

(5) Non-English community sources -- searched Chinese (Zhihu, CSDN), Korean, and Japanese queries. Found substantial Chinese hands-on-testing coverage (Zhihu long-form reviews, CSDN posts) but all either repeat the vendor's own tracked benchmarks (MMLU-Pro, AIME, GPQA, SWE-Bench) or discuss qualitative usage impressions; the one full-text-verified technical-summary article (see arXiv discussion above) explicitly confirms the source paper's benchmark list excludes base MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. Some search-engine result summaries floated specific numbers ('MMLU 89.2', 'MATH-500 85.6%') attributed to Zhihu articles, but direct fetch-and-read of those articles' full text did not contain those figures anywhere -- these read as search-summarization artifacts/hallucinations rather than real published numbers, and were not used.

(6) Sibling models -- the immediate predecessor, Kimi K2 (0711), does have MMLU=89.5 and MATH=97.4 (already known/superseded per this entry's existing verificationNotes), confirming Moonshot possessed these eval pipelines but deliberately dropped them starting with K2.5/K2.6/K2-Thinking in favor of MMLU-Pro and Omni-MATH/AIME-style successors. HELM Capabilities' 'Kimi K2 Instruct' sibling row likewise reports MMLU-Pro/Omni-MATH only, no base MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. No sibling-model paper or comparison table with a genuine, sourced K2.5 base-MMLU/MATH/HumanEval figure was located.

Conclusion: all 6 new source types were checked (exceeding the 3-type minimum) and none yielded a credible, citable score for mmlu, math, or humaneval for Kimi K2.5. This corroborates and extends the prior pass's finding: Moonshot's K2.5+ generation appears to have permanently retired these three legacy/saturated benchmarks from its own reporting, and no independent evaluator (HELM, arXiv baseline tables, GitHub harness runs, other leaderboards, or non-English community testing) has filled the gap either. Re-affirming omitted_no_evidence for all three; no scores added this round (results array unchanged from the prior pass's 20 entries).
